title: Viewport Navigation
learningTarget: I can navigate the Unreal viewport confidently using mouse controls, keyboard movement, focus, view modes, show flags, and game view.

bellRinger.prompt: Why might navigation controls matter before building anything in a 3D scene?

vocabulary.definition: The controls used to move the editor camera around the 3D world.
A first-person style movement method used while holding the right mouse button.
The setting that controls how fast the viewport camera moves.
The F key action that snaps the viewport to the selected object.
A viewport display mode such as Lit, Unlit, or Wireframe.
A viewport option toggled with G that hides editor-only widgets and shows what the player would see.

assignment.title: Viewport Navigation Scavenger Hunt
evidenceRequired: Screenshots from at least three different views or camera positions
One screenshot showing game view or a different view mode

exitTicket: What does pressing G toggle in the viewport?
